title: Add mute and unmute control
overview: Create a visible mute/unmute toggle button that controls audio gain via the existing AudioPlayer.setMuted() method. The button follows the established UI pattern from infoOverlay.ts and is mounted in main.ts after the audio player initialises. Audio analysis continues while muted because the AnalyserNode sits before the GainNode in the pipeline.

steps.description: Create the mute toggle module following the same vanilla-DOM pattern used in src/ui/infoOverlay.ts. Export a single factory function createMuteButton(player: AudioPlayer): HTMLButtonElement that creates a fixed-position button, reads player.muted for initial label, and toggles via player.setMuted() on click. The button text/aria-label updates on each click to reflect current state (muted vs unmuted).
details: Import AudioPlayer type from src/audio/player.ts
Export function createMuteButton(player: AudioPlayer): HTMLButtonElement
Use document.createElement('button') with className 'eavi-mute-btn'
Set aria-label to 'Unmute audio' or 'Mute audio' based on player.muted
Use a speaker icon character for visual label (e.g. '🔇' muted, '🔊' unmuted — or simple text if emoji is avoided)
On click: call player.setMuted(!player.muted), then update button text and aria-label
No module-level state needed — player.muted is the source of truth

steps.description: Add styles for the mute button matching the existing .eavi-info-btn pattern: fixed position, bottom-left to avoid collision with the info button at bottom-right, 44px minimum tap target, semi-transparent background with backdrop blur, z-index 10.
details: Position: fixed, bottom: 1.5rem, left: 1.5rem (mirrors info button but on the left)
Same z-index (10), min-width/min-height (44px), border-radius, background, color, cursor, backdrop-filter, and transition as .eavi-info-btn
Add hover state matching .eavi-info-btn:hover
Add prefers-reduced-motion rule to disable transition

steps.action: Wire up the mute button in src/main.ts
steps.description: Import createMuteButton from src/ui/audioToggle.ts. Inside the existing audioPromise.then() callback, create the button by passing the resolved player and append it to document.body. This is straightforward — no existing DOM-append pattern is needed because main.ts currently only logs.
details: Add import: import { createMuteButton } from './ui/audioToggle'
Inside audioPromise.then((player) => { ... }): call createMuteButton(player) and append the returned element to document.body
Keep the existing console.debug log

steps.description: Create tests/ui/audioToggle.test.ts verifying: button is created with correct initial state (muted), clicking toggles player.setMuted and updates aria-label, and that the button element has the expected CSS class.
details: Create a mock AudioPlayer with muted=true and a setMuted spy that toggles the mocked muted property
Test: createMuteButton returns an HTMLButtonElement with class 'eavi-mute-btn'
Test: initial aria-label reflects muted state ('Unmute audio')
Test: clicking the button calls setMuted(false) and updates aria-label to 'Mute audio'
Test: clicking again calls setMuted(true) and reverts aria-label

steps.description: Run npm run build to confirm no type errors or build failures. Manually verify: the button is visible during the scene, toggles gain instantly, remains accessible, and audio analysis data continues flowing while muted (already guaranteed by the pipeline architecture where AnalyserNode precedes GainNode).
details: npm run build passes
No console errors on load
No forbidden storage APIs (cookies, localStorage)
Scene works with and without autoplay
Mute toggle is visible and functional
Audio analysis continues while muted (structural guarantee from analyser.ts pipeline)

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.A visible control toggles audio gain: Steps 1-3: createMuteButton creates a visible fixed-position button that calls player.setMuted(), which sets gainNode.gain.value to 0 or 1
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Toggle updates instantly: Step 1: Click handler calls player.setMuted() which directly sets gainNode.gain.value — no animation or fade, instant effect
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Control is accessible while the scene is: Step 2: Button uses position:fixed with z-index:10, ensuring it floats above the canvas at all times
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Audio analysis continues while muted: Already satisfied by existing architecture in src/audio/analyser.ts — the pipeline is source→AnalyserNode→GainNode→destination, so analysis data flows regardless of gain value. No changes needed.

reviewerFeedbackResolution: Addressed: Step 3 no longer references an info button pattern. The plan simply appends the mute button to document.body inside the existing audioPromise.then() callback, which is straightforward and does not depend on any other UI mounting.

risks.risk: Emoji rendering inconsistency across platforms for speaker icons
risks.mitigation: Use simple Unicode text characters or SVG path instead of emoji if cross-platform consistency is important
